c410a272-cac2-486d-9911-a5a73f5365d2.name: Classic Exploration
c410a272-cac2-486d-9911-a5a73f5365d2.body_tagged:
A journey through ancient capitals, sacred sites, and wild landscapes [R:atom_af8135bbec][R:atom_bf430acfe9][R:atom_95989beb2b]
Begin in Negombo, where the fishing village awakens with catamarans lined along the shore [R:atom_300afd2b5f]. Travel through Sri Lanka’s cultural heartland, exploring Anuradhapura’s Sacred Bo Tree, the rock-cut temples of Dambulla, and the monolithic Buddha at Aukana [R:atom_af8135bbec][R:atom_c60d215194][R:atom_990427e559]. Climb Sigiriya at dawn, passing frescoed galleries on the way to the summit [R:atom_62c6d7e62d]. In Polonnaruwa, walk among the towering stone figures of Gal Vihara [R:atom_bf430acfe9]. Witness elephants up close at Pinnawela Elephant Orphanage and in Minneriya National Park [R:atom_dd4305536d][R:atom_95989beb2b]. Continue to Kandy to visit the Temple of the Tooth and attend a traditional dance performance [R:atom_115412286c][R:atom_17df7a9aa6]. Journey through tea country, from Nuwara Eliya’s colonial streets to the misty train ride to Ella and the viewpoint at Lipton’s Seat [R:atom_8467958b00][R:atom_ff862e9320][R:atom_31abab626c][R:atom_31abab626c]. Explore Yala and Kataragama before concluding with Galle’s Dutch Fort and stilt fishermen [R:atom_be9728afb9][R:atom_9c549a8a09][R:atom_c4784fa0e2].
Climb Sigiriya Lion's Rock at first light, discovering ancient frescoes en route to the summit [R:atom_62c6d7e62d]
Walk among the 22-meter-long reclining Buddha and other stone carvings at Gal Vihara in Polonnaruwa [R:atom_bf430acfe9]
Take a jeep safari in Minneriya National Park to observe wild elephant herds in their natural habitat [R:atom_95989beb2b]
Ride the scenic 'train on clouds' from Nanu Oya to Ella, drifting through tea plantations and mountain mist [R:atom_ff862e9320]
Visit the Temple of the Tooth Relic in Kandy, a center of Buddhist veneration for centuries [R:atom_115412286c]
Day 1: Arrive in Negombo and visit the fishing village in the morning, where catamarans line the shore and daily life unfolds by the sea [R:atom_300afd2b5f]. Day 2: Travel to Anuradhapura and explore the ancient capital, including the Sacred Bo Tree, one of the world’s oldest living religious monuments [R:atom_af8135bbec]. Day 3: Visit Mihintale, recognized as the world’s first declared sanctuary, before proceeding to the colossal rock-cut Buddha at Aukana [R:atom_7582f0eb9a][R:atom_990427e559]. Day 4: Begin early with a climb up Sigiriya Lion's Rock, examining its frescoed galleries before reaching the summit [R:atom_62c6d7e62d]. Day 5: Walk through the Polonnaruwa Archaeological Site, including the monumental Gal Vihara statues carved into the rock face [R:atom_bf430acfe9]. Day 6: Take a jeep safari in Minneriya National Park to observe wild elephants gathering near the reservoir [R:atom_95989beb2b]. Day 7: Visit Pinnawela Elephant Orphanage to see baby elephants being bottle-fed and bathed in the river [R:atom_dd4305536d]. Day 8: Stop at the Dambulla Cave Temple complex, entering sacred caves adorned with religious art, then continue to Kandy [R:atom_c60d215194]. Day 9: Visit the Temple of the Tooth Relic, a major pilgrimage site in Buddhism, and attend a cultural dance show in the evening [R:atom_115412286c][R:atom_17df7a9aa6]. Day 10: Stroll through the Peradeniya Botanical Gardens before traveling to Nuwara Eliya, passing through tea plantations [R:atom_4d7b83cdc3][R:atom_8467958b00]. Day 11: Take the scenic train ride from Nanu Oya to Ella, known as the 'train on clouds,' moving through misty hills and tea estates [R:atom_ff862e9320]. Day 12: Hike Little Adam's Peak for sweeping views of Ella’s surroundings, then reach Lipton's Seat by tuk-tuk, passing working tea plantations [R:atom_c5dc90c655][R:atom_31abab626c]. Day 13: Stop at Ravana Falls en route to Tissamaharama [R:atom_3da8b3a98f]. Day 14: Begin with a jeep safari in Yala National Park, searching for elephants, leopards, and other wildlife [R:atom_be9728afb9]. Day 15: Visit the sacred Kataragama Temple in the evening, experiencing the atmosphere of a living pilgrimage site [R:atom_9c549a8a09]. Day 16: Visit Galle’s Dutch Fort and observe traditional stilt fishermen balancing on poles offshore [R:atom_c4784fa0e2].

text_by_id.atom_dd4305536d: At Pinnawela Elephant Orphanage, observe baby elephants being bottle-fed and bathed in the river.

text_by_id.atom_17df7a9aa6: Attend a cultural dance show in Kandy in the evening.
text_by_id.atom_8467958b00: Travel through tea plantations to Nuwara Eliya, often called 'Little England,' and stroll through its colonial town.
text_by_id.atom_ff862e9320: Take a scenic train ride from Nanu Oya to Ella, known as the 'train on clouds,' through tea plantations.
text_by_id.atom_31abab626c: Reach Lipton's Seat viewpoint by tuk-tuk, passing scenic tea plantations and local life along the way.
text_by_id.atom_be9728afb9: Start the day with a jeep safari in Yala National Park to spot elephants, leopards, and more.

text_by_id.atom_7582f0eb9a: Visit Mihintale, described as the world's first declared sanctuary.

cc2eca43-5422-4783-8a60-53cf0aef3003.name: Classic Exploration
cc2eca43-5422-4783-8a60-53cf0aef3003.body_tagged:
A journey through ancient capitals, sacred sites, and wild landscapes [R:atom_cef7525c63][R:atom_114dda222e][R:atom_b20cfd6dc8]
Begin in Negombo Fishing Village, where traditional catamarans line the shore and the morning bustle offers an authentic first impression of Sri Lankan coastal life before the tourist trail begins [R:atom_79b86b7813]. Explore Anuradhapura, the ancient Buddhist capital, walking among ruins of one of Asia's oldest civilisations, including the Sacred Bo Tree and monastic remains [R:atom_cef7525c63]. Climb Sigiriya Lion's Rock at sunrise, ascending an ancient sky fortress to discover stunning frescoes and sweeping views [R:atom_ebc7512930]. In Polonnaruwa, stand before the monumental rock-cut Buddha statues of Gal Vihara, a sight that stops you mid-step [R:atom_114dda222e]. Witness wild elephants gathering at Minneriya National Park on a safari that captures one of Sri Lanka's signature wildlife spectacles [R:atom_3c686293ac]. Continue to Kandy to visit the Temple of the Tooth Relic, one of Buddhism's most sacred shrines, where reverence is palpable [R:atom_e28f55f8de], and attend an evening cultural dance show featuring vivid costumes and drumming [R:atom_4b38e24e02]. Travel through tea country with a scenic train ride from Nanu Oya to Ella, often described as the 'train on clouds,' winding through mist-wrapped hills [R:atom_7a3e483f62]. End with a jeep safari in Yala National Park, where the high density of leopards keeps every moment charged with possibility [R:atom_b20cfd6dc8].
Sunrise ascent of Sigiriya Lion's Rock, a UNESCO site with ancient frescoes and panoramic views [R:atom_ebc7512930]
Standing before the Aukana Buddha, carved from a single unbroken rock face at Mihintale, the world's first declared sanctuary [R:atom_1d0078d0da]
Watching baby elephants splash and play during feeding and bathing at Pinnawela Elephant Orphanage [R:atom_c1d0a3942d]
Tuk-tuk ride through tea plantations to Lipton's Seat, arriving at a sweeping highland panorama [R:atom_f2b102ab9a]
Evening rituals at Kataragama Temple, one of Sri Lanka's most revered pilgrimage sites [R:atom_b3d9be073e]
Day 1 begins with a visit to Negombo Fishing Village, where traditional catamarans line the shore and the morning vibrancy offers an authentic first impression of Sri Lankan coastal life [R:atom_79b86b7813]. On Day 2, explore Anuradhapura, the ancient Buddhist capital, including the Sacred Bo Tree and monastic ruins [R:atom_cef7525c63], and visit Mihintale, home to the Aukana Buddha statue carved from a single rock [R:atom_1d0078d0da]. Day 3 starts early with a climb up Sigiriya Lion's Rock, a UNESCO site featuring stunning frescoes [R:atom_ebc7512930], followed by exploration of Polonnaruwa's archaeological treasures, including the Gal Vihara Buddha statues [R:atom_114dda222e], and concludes with a safari in Minneriya National Park to witness wild elephants in their natural habitat [R:atom_3c686293ac]. Day 4 includes an optional morning whale-watching excursion from Trincomalee [R:atom_357e975c8d], then a stop at the Dambulla Cave Temple en route to Kandy [R:atom_8f2991a840]. On Day 5, visit the Temple of the Tooth Relic in Kandy, one of Buddhism's most sacred shrines [R:atom_e28f55f8de] and attend an evening cultural dance show [R:atom_4b38e24e02]. Day 6 includes a visit to Peradeniya Botanical Gardens [R:atom_8fa87ef845] before boarding a scenic train ride from Nanu Oya to Ella, traversing mist-wrapped tea hills [R:atom_7a3e483f62]. Day 7 features a tuk-tuk journey to Lipton's Seat, passing through tea plantations and local life [R:atom_f2b102ab9a], with a stop at Ravana Falls en route to Tissamaharama [R:atom_c237ce1ecd]. Day 8 includes an evening visit to the sacred Kataragama Temple [R:atom_b3d9be073e] and a jeep safari in Yala National Park to spot leopards, elephants, and other wildlife [R:atom_b20cfd6dc8]. Day 9 brings a visit to Galle's historical Dutch Fort and observation of traditional stilt fishermen along the coast [R:atom_19814a936b].

text_by_id.atom_114dda222e: Explore Polonnaruwa's archaeological treasures, including the Gal Vihara Buddha statues.
text_by_id.atom_b20cfd6dc8: Jeep safari in Yala National Park to spot leopards, elephants, and more.
text_by_id.atom_79b86b7813: Visit Negombo Fishing Village on arrival morning, known for its morning vibrancy and lined-up traditional catamarans.

text_by_id.atom_3c686293ac: Conclude Day 3 with a safari in Minneriya National Park to witness wild elephants in their natural habitat.
text_by_id.atom_e28f55f8de: Visit the Temple of the Tooth Relic in Kandy, one of Buddhism's most sacred shrines.
text_by_id.atom_4b38e24e02: Evening cultural dance show in Kandy.
text_by_id.atom_7a3e483f62: Take a scenic train ride from Nanu Oya to Ella, described as the 'train on clouds,' traversing tea country.
text_by_id.atom_1d0078d0da: Visit Mihintale, described as the world's first declared sanctuary, and the Aukana Buddha statue carved from a single rock.
text_by_id.atom_357e975c8d: Optional morning whale-watching excursion from Trincomalee before continuing inland.

text_by_id.atom_8fa87ef845: Explore the Peradeniya Botanical Gardens before heading up through tea plantations toward Nuwara Eliya.
text_by_id.atom_f2b102ab9a: Reach Lipton's Seat viewpoint by tuk-tuk, passing through scenic tea plantations and local life.
text_by_id.atom_c237ce1ecd: Stop at Ravana Falls before arriving in Tissamaharama.
text_by_id.atom_b3d9be073e: Evening visit to the sacred Kataragama Temple in Tissamaharama.
text_by_id.atom_19814a936b: Visit Galle's historical Dutch Fort and observe traditional stilt fishermen along the coast.
text_by_id.atom_c1d0a3942d: At Pinnawela Elephant Orphanage, observe baby elephants being bottle-fed and bathed in the river.

a3afaed4-4492-45d7-9a41-ff60314d4439.name: A sea of coral, Prayers of Uminchu fishermen
a3afaed4-4492-45d7-9a41-ff60314d4439.body_tagged:
Sail with traditional boat builders and fishers who read the sea like a map [R:atom_5dc2f5cf6b][R:atom_0b66f7bfa1]
This journey unfolds along the coastlines and currents of Okinawa, where the sea is not just a resource but a living thread of culture and myth. You’ll visit a working atelier where wooden sabani sailboats are crafted using methods passed down through generations, and speak with shipbuilders dedicated to keeping this knowledge alive [R:atom_5dc2f5cf6b]. In quiet coastal villages, you’ll walk with guides whose lives still follow the rhythms of the tides, experiencing how ocean-based customs endure in daily life [R:atom_91705a9c13]. The trip brings you to Cape Hedo, the northern edge of Okinawa, where sheer limestone cliffs rise above cobalt waters and the reef stretches toward Yoron Island — a vantage point few travelers ever reach [R:atom_d177b30793]. Behind these cliffs lies a sacred utaki site, believed in Ryukyu tradition as the landing place of Amamikiyo, the sea goddess who shaped the islands themselves [R:atom_f0b59064bc]. You’ll kayak from a working fishing port to a reef-fringed islet, learning from uminchu fishermen how to interpret sea depth, tides, and fish signs as their ancestors did [R:atom_0b66f7bfa1]. And in a rare act of participation, you’ll plant coral fragments alongside these fishers, then snorkel above the reef you helped restore — a moment where conservation becomes personal [R:atom_cb406749ef].
Sail aboard a traditionally built sabani after meeting the artisans who keep the craft alive [R:atom_5dc2f5cf6b]
Kayak with uminchu fishermen and learn to read the sea as they have for centuries [R:atom_0b66f7bfa1]
Plant coral directly into the reef and snorkel over the living ecosystem you’ve helped renew [R:atom_cb406749ef]
Stand at Cape Hedo’s cliffs, where myth and geography meet at the edge of Okinawa [R:atom_d177b30793][R:atom_f0b59064bc]
Walk coastal villages shaped entirely by the sea, where tradition and daily life remain intertwined [R:atom_91705a9c13]
On one day, you visit a manufacturing atelier for wooden sabani sailboats, speaking with shipbuilders committed to passing on their craft, then sail out onto the open sea [R:atom_5dc2f5cf6b]. Another day takes you through coastal villages where ocean-related customs remain part of everyday life, guided by locals whose routines are still set by the sea’s rhythms [R:atom_91705a9c13]. You kayak from a fishing port to a reef-encircled island, learning directly from uminchu fishermen how to interpret sea depth, tides, fish species, and seasonal patterns [R:atom_0b66f7bfa1]. You join those same fishermen in planting coral by hand, then snorkel above the reef, seeing the living results of your contribution [R:atom_cb406749ef]. At Cape Hedo, you stand atop limestone cliffs at the northern tip of Okinawa, facing Yoron Island across vivid blue waters and looking down on thriving coral reefs [R:atom_d177b30793]. Behind the cliffs, you visit a sacred utaki site — the place in Ryukyu belief where the sea goddess Amamikiyo first stepped onto the islands she created [R:atom_f0b59064bc].

text_by_id.atom_5dc2f5cf6b: Visit a manufacturing atelier of wooden sabani sailboats and speak with shipbuilders working to pass traditional building techniques to future generations, then sail out into the wide sea.
text_by_id.atom_0b66f7bfa1: Kayak from a fishing port to an island in a shoal surrounded by reefs, learning uminchu wisdom such as reading sea depth, tides, fish species, and seasonal information.
text_by_id.atom_91705a9c13: Explore coastal villages where customs related to the ocean remain present in today's way of life, guided through settlements where daily rhythms still align with the sea.
text_by_id.atom_d177b30793: Stand on Cape Hedo's sheer limestone cliffs at the northern tip of Okinawa, looking out to Yoron island in the distance and down to coral reefs in cobalt blue water.
text_by_id.atom_f0b59064bc: At Cape Hedo, a sacred utaki site stands behind the cliffs — the place in Ryukyu creation myth where the sea goddess Amamikiyo, who created the islands of Ryukyu, first landed.
text_by_id.atom_cb406749ef: Join uminchu fishermen to plant coral with your own hands, then snorkel above the living reef — not just witnessing it, but having played a role in its future.

296abe67-80e6-43c8-81af-2088b111d31f.name: Yaksa Trek BEST DEAL
296abe67-80e6-43c8-81af-2088b111d31f.body_tagged:
A multi-day trek through the remote Yaksa region, culminating in a night under the stars [R:atom_83c773a60a]
This trek unfolds across the rugged Yaksa region, where each day builds toward a sustained wilderness experience and an overnight camp beneath the open sky [R:atom_83c773a60a]. After the ascent and time spent immersed in the backcountry, the descent leads to Thimphu, offering quiet reflection before the flight home [R:atom_5e1931b515].
Dedicated trekking days that progressively immerse you in the remote Yaksa landscape [R:atom_83c773a60a]
An overnight camp under the stars, the culmination of the trekking journey [R:atom_83c773a60a]
Time in Thimphu following the descent, allowing a gradual return from wilderness to city [R:atom_5e1931b515]
The trek includes multiple dedicated days on foot, moving deeper into the Yaksa region with each stage, building toward the central experience of camping in the wild [R:atom_83c773a60a]. The final trekking day is a descent from the high country, after which travelers spend time in Thimphu before flying home, marking the quiet close of the journey [R:atom_5e1931b515].

text_by_id.atom_83c773a60a: Multi-day trek through the Yaksa region, with dedicated trekking days building up to a camp night.
text_by_id.atom_5e1931b515: Descent day followed by time in Thimphu before flying home.
